description_long: This structure is a small, elevated pavilion with a wide, gently sloping hip roof made of light-colored material, supported by slender posts. The main body is constructed from bamboo or wood, featuring a wraparound balcony with railings and an open entrance at the front. The roof is a single, continuous tier with straight eaves and no visible decorative finial at the apex. The building is raised above the ground on four posts, providing an airy, open space beneath. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on natural materials and open-air accessibility. This type of structure is commonly found in parks or gardens, serving as a shaded resting spot or lookout.
description: A small, elevated bamboo pavilion with a wide, single-tier hip roof and open balcony, designed for shade and relaxation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open entrance, balcony railings, and the full width of the hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the balcony and the supporting posts beneath the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view with the balcony railing visible and the roof's overhang clearly shown.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the structure's symmetry and open design.
